evals.prompt: I built an open-source Rust CLI tool and want to share it on Lobsters. How should I approach this?
evals.expected_output: Recommends getting an invite via the invitation queue or a known member, spending time participating in the community before posting, using the release tag plus relevant technical tags like rust and cli, and keeping self-promotion under 25% of total activity.

assertions.name: invite_process
assertions.description: Explains how to get a Lobsters invitation, mentioning the invitation queue or reaching out to existing members

assertions.name: community_participation
assertions.description: Advises participating in the community (commenting, sharing others' work) before self-promoting, and mentions the less-than-25-percent self-promotion guideline

assertions.name: tagging_and_submission
assertions.description: Recommends specific tags for the submission (such as release, rust, or cli) and advises writing a substantive description rather than just dropping a link

evals.prompt: What's the difference between Lobsters and Hacker News for launching a developer tool?
evals.expected_output: Compares invite-only vs open, traffic levels, audience focus, marketing tolerance, and self-promotion norms. Notes HN has much more traffic but Lobsters has higher signal-to-noise and stricter anti-marketing culture.

assertions.name: access_comparison
assertions.description: Explains that Lobsters is invite-only while Hacker News has open registration, and notes the significant traffic difference between the two platforms

assertions.name: marketing_tolerance
assertions.description: Describes Lobsters' stricter anti-marketing culture compared to HN, mentioning that commercial content is more likely to be flagged on Lobsters

assertions.name: recommendation
assertions.description: Provides a recommendation or framework for choosing between the platforms based on the type of product (open-source vs commercial) or the user's goals

evals.prompt: I just got a Lobsters invite. What are the rules I need to know as a new member?
evals.expected_output: Covers the 70-day new user restrictions, the less-than-25-percent self-promotion rule, the tagging system, the anti-marketing culture, and the public invitation tree accountability system.

assertions.name: new_user_restrictions
assertions.description: Explains the 70-day new user restriction period and what new members cannot do during that time, such as submitting from new domains or flagging content

assertions.name: self_promotion_rule
assertions.description: Describes the self-promotion guideline that it should be less than a quarter of total contributions

assertions.name: community_norms
assertions.description: Covers at least one important community norm beyond self-promotion, such as the anti-marketing culture, the public mod log, the tagging requirements, or the accountability through the invitation tree
